mechanism_narrative: A sustained quarterly increase in real GDP (GDPC1) signals strong economic demand, which boosts corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth fails to correlate with 60-day SPX returns (Rank IC < 0.05) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPIAUCSL reading above 220 indicates elevated inflation, prompting central bank tightening expectations that depress equity valuations and generate negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns remain positive in 60%+ of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 220.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent monthly growth in industrial production (INDPRO) and payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals robust supply-side expansion, creating positive momentum in S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: The interaction between INDPRO and PAYEMS fails to explain SPX returns (p > 0.10 in joint regression) across 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in unemployment (UNRATE) below 5% combined with rising payroll gains (PAYEMS) indicates tight labor markets, driving positive S&P 500 returns during late-cycle expansions.
failure_condition: SPX returns are negative in 50%+ of periods where UNRATE < 5% and PAYEMS > 100k.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly housing starts (HOUST) above 600k signal strong demand for construction, boosting related sectors and generating positive S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are negative in 60%+ of instances where HOUST exceeds 600k.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly rise in industrial production (INDPRO) above 101.0 indicates expanding manufacturing activity, which drives positive S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict 5-day SPX returns (Rank IC < 0.03) over 2-year periods.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of low federal funds rates (FEDFUNDS < 0.5%) and rising CPI (CPIAUCSL > 218) signals accommodative policy amid inflation, creating mixed equity returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are positive in 50%+ of periods where FEDFUNDS < 0.5% and CPIAUCSL > 218.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous monthly growth in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) reflects strong consumer and business demand, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 60 days.
failure_condition: The joint movement of RSAFS and HOUST fails to explain SPX returns (p > 0.15 in joint regression) across 3-year windows.
